Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Deep Learning Optimization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can significantly improve the stability and efficiency of deep learning optimization by dynamically adjusting precision levels based on the curvature of the loss landscape, leading to faster convergence and reduced memory usage.
Related Work: While existing work has explored the use of deep reinforcement learning for routing in networking (Alenazi et al., 2025) and graph-aware deep learning for routing (Zhuang et al., 2018), our proposal focuses on the curvature-aware precision routing in the context of deep learning optimization. Previous work on precision-adaptive optimization (Sheibanian et al., 2025) and curvature-aware methods (Bai et al., 2026) provides a foundation, but our approach integrates these concepts in a novel way to address the specific challenges of deep learning optimization, such as numerical stability and computational efficiency.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for deep learning optimization that dynamically adjusts the precision levels of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape. This approach aims to improve the stability and efficiency of training by reducing the computational overhead and memory usage while maintaining or even enhancing model accuracy. The framework uses a combination of spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation to ensure that the precision adjustments are both computationally feasible and theoretically grounded. We validate our approach through a series of experiments on popular deep learning tasks, demonstrating significant improvements in convergence speed and resource utilization.
Experiments: Evaluate the curvature-aware precision routing framework on a variety of deep learning tasks, including image classification (e.g., CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100) and natural language processing (e.g., GLUE benchmarks).
Compare the performance of the proposed framework against existing mixed-precision training methods and full-precision baselines in terms of convergence speed, memory usage, and final model accuracy.
Conduct ablation studies to analyze the impact of different components of the framework, such as spectral quantization, curvature estimation, and precision adjustment strategies.
Perform a detailed analysis of the numerical stability and computational overhead of the curvature-aware precision routing framework, ensuring that it remains feasible for single-GPU deployments.
Use formal PAC-Bayes verification to establish theoretical bounds on the generalization performance of models trained with the proposed framework.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The curvature estimation process may introduce additional computational overhead, which could offset the benefits of precision routing. We will carefully optimize this process to ensure it remains lightweight.
The dynamic adjustment of precision levels might lead to instability if not properly managed. We will implement robust diagnostic loops to monitor and mitigate such issues.
The framework's performance may vary across different types of deep learning models and tasks. We will conduct extensive testing to ensure its broad applicability.

The proposed approach relies on the assumption that curvature information is a good indicator for precision adjustments. We will validate this assumption through empirical studies and theoretical analysis.
